name: operator-clarity
mandate: Can an agent execute Steps 0-3 unambiguously? Where would it have to GUESS — is the literal token <skill> ever resolved to a real path; does the agent know the absolute script paths; are pwsh invocation details correct on Windows? Ambiguity here means the enforced flow never gets invoked correctly.

name: doc-code-consistency
mandate: Does the SKILL.md accurately describe what the bundled scripts actually do and which flags/params they take? Any claim in the prose that the scripts do NOT deliver (or vice versa) is a defect — drift between manual and machine.

name: gate-bypass
mandate: This is a skill ABOUT enforcement that is itself invoked via prose. Can an agent read this and still skip the gate, hand-set depth, or synthesize without the orchestrator? Find the residual prose-only seams where the enforcement depends on the agent choosing to comply.

name: failure-modes
mandate: Are the degraded/failure paths complete and honest? Mode A disclosure, the unattended path, ok:false handling, partial artifacts, missing scripts, the symlink/base-dir resolution. How does this go wrong in real use?

name: prior-art
mandate: Does this follow established skill-authoring and operator-manual conventions (Anthropic skill-creator guidance, progressive disclosure, description triggering)? Is anything here reinventing or contradicting settled practice?
